title: Pokémon Bursts onto the Gaming Scene
story: Gotta catch em all! On February 27, 1996, Pokémon made its electrifying debut in Japan. The role-playing video game Pocket Monsters Red and Green launched for the Game Boy, introducing players to a world of adorable creatures with amazing powers. Trainers could capture, train, and battle with their Pokémon, starting a global phenomenon that would soon capture hearts worldwide.
activity_question: What type of video game was the first Pokémon game?
bonus_fact: The original Pokémon games were called Red and Green in Japan, but Red and Blue internationally.

title: Black Hole's Colossal Cosmic Blast
story: Boom! The universe put on a spectacular light show in 2020. Astronomers detected the biggest cosmic explosion ever seen, coming from a supermassive black hole. This massive burst happened in the Ophiuchus galaxy, nearly 390 million light-years from Earth. The explosion was so enormous it could fit about 15 Milky Way galaxies inside it! Scientists shared their amazing discovery in the Astrophysical Journal on February 27.
activity_question: How far away was the galaxy where this massive explosion occurred?
follow_up_question: What causes explosions in space?

title: Rare Twins Surprise Doctors in Australia

This plea marked the beginning of a decades-long effort to save one of Italy's most beloved landmarks.

title: Unlocking the Secrets of Time
story: Scientists made a groundbreaking discovery in 1940 that would change how we understand history. On February 27, Martin Kamen and Sam Ruben found a special form of carbon called carbon-14 at the University of California. This tiny atom became the key to radiocarbon dating, a method that helps researchers figure out how old ancient objects are. It's like giving archaeologists a time machine!

story: Angelina Weld Grimke burst onto the scene on February 27, 1880. Born in Boston, this talented woman would grow up to become a shining light of the Harlem Renaissance. Grimke wore many hats as a journalist, teacher, and playwright. Her words and ideas helped shape an important cultural movement that celebrated African American art and literature.
activity_question: What three roles did Angelina Weld Grimke have?

title: Math Whiz Born: Bertus Brouwers
story: A brilliant mind entered the world on February 27, 1881. Bertus Brouwers, born in Overschie, Netherlands, would grow up to become a math superstar. This Dutch thinker didn't just crunch numbers - he revolutionized how we understand shapes and space. Brouwers founded modern topology, a branch of math that explores stretchy, twisty objects. His ideas still boggle minds today!
activity_question: What branch of mathematics did Bertus Brouwers found?
follow_up_question: Can you think of any objects that can be stretched or twisted without breaking?
